mostFrequentlyAskedAmendments: 42nd Amendment Act (1976) - Focus on Preamble changes, Fundamental Duties, and 'Mini-Constitution' status
44th Amendment Act (1978) - Focus on right to property shift (Art 300A), armed rebellion substitution, and emergency safeguards
73rd & 74th Amendment Acts (1992) - Panchayati Raj (Part IX) and Municipalities (Part IX-A)
86th Amendment Act (2002) - Right to Education under Article 21A
mostFrequentlyAskedArticles: Article 368 - Power of Parliament to amend the Constitution and procedure
Article 352 - Proclamation of National Emergency (grounds, majority, and duration)
Articles 358 & 359 - Suspension of Fundamental Rights, specifically highlighting Articles 20 and 21
repeatedThemes: Comparison between National Emergency (1 month / special majority) and President's Rule approval timelines (2 months / simple majority)
The untouchable status of Articles 20 and 21 during any emergency proclamation
Landmark case laws associated with amendments, particularly the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) and the Basic Structure doctrine
Substitution of terms, such as 'internal disturbance' being replaced by 'armed rebellion' via the 44th Amendment
difficultyTrend: Shifting from direct 1-line factual recall of article numbers or years toward multi-statement matching questions (e.g., checking correct combinations of emergency impacts or specific amendment objectives in Tier-2 exams).
highProbabilityQuestions: Which Constitutional Amendment Act introduced the 10% EWS reservation in educational institutions?
What is the maximum duration for which President's Rule can be extended under normal parliamentary approvals with periodic renewals?
Which specific articles are permanently shielded from suspension during a National Emergency under post-44th Amendment rules?
expectedFutureAreas: Recent landmark amendments like the 101st (GST), 102nd (NCBC), 103rd (EWS), 104th (Anglo-Indian seats discontinuation & SC/ST extension), and 105th Amendments
Nuances of state ratification requirements under Article 368 proviso for federal provisions
Procedural checks on executive misuse of emergency powers introduced post-1978
